- ChatScreen: add LaunchedEffect(isGenerating) with snapshotFlow to scroll during
  streaming; only scrolls if user is near the bottom (lastIndex - lastVisible <= 2)

  heuristic (endsWith '...') reliably fires for short first messages too

* fix(#139): backtick-wrapped URLs render as clickable links not code spans

The model frequently wraps bare URLs in backticks. The inline code regex ran
before URL detection, so the CODE span (starting at the backtick) won
deduplication and the URL appeared as monospace code. Fix: detect when a
code span's trimmed content starts with http://, https://, or www. and emit
a URL span instead.

Closes #139

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix code review agent timeouts caused by gpt-5.4 (#126783)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was developed with Copilot assistance based on analysis of
workflow run logs and duration data.

## Problem

~20% of custom code review agent runs hit the 20-minute workflow
timeout. Analysis of all 43 timeout runs from the last 1000 workflow
executions shows:

- **93% of timeouts** are caused by GPT-5.4 sub-agents that never return
- GPT-5.4 is present in **100% of timeout runs** (24/24 checked in
detail)
- GPT-5.2-only runs have **0 timeouts** in 6+ successful runs
- 86% of timed-out runs had already posted the review — they time out
waiting for hung sub-agents
- Each timeout causes the agent job to fail, making the overall workflow
**red in CI** (even though the `conclusion` job succeeds) — PR authors
must manually rerun

The current SKILL.md rule "pick the highest version number" causes
gpt-5.4 to always be selected when available.

## Changes (SKILL.md only)

1. **Block gpt-5.4** — it has known reliability issues. Recommend
`gpt-5.3-codex` as the GPT-family pick instead. If that also exhibits
hangs, we can block the GPT family entirely with no expected quality
loss.
2. **Exit after posting** — the agent was lingering 2-3 minutes after
successfully posting the review comment, waiting for hung sub-agents.
Now it exits immediately once the comment is visible.
3. **Reduce max sub-agents from 4 to 3** — with only 2-3 model families
available in practice, 4 was never fully utilized.

## What this does NOT change

- The 10-minute sub-agent timeout instruction (already in place,
appropriate for agents that do return)
- The overall workflow `timeout-minutes: 20` (hardcoded in the compiled
`.lock.yml`)
- The review methodology, severity definitions, or quality bar
- Any CCR (Copilot Code Review) configuration

## Expected impact

- Eliminates the dominant timeout cause (GPT-5.4 hangs)
- Saves 2-3 min per run from exit-after-post
- No expected quality regression: GPT contributed unique blocking
findings in 0% of sampled runs

## Data

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Runs analyzed | 1000 workflow runs, 420 non-skipped, 218 with CLI data
|
| Timeout rate | 19.7% (43/218) |
| GPT-5.4 in timeouts | 100% (24/24 detailed) |
| GPT-5.2 timeouts | 0% (0/6+ successful GPT-5.2 runs) |
| Reviews with GPT-unique findings | <8% |
| GPT-only blocking bugs found | 0 |
| MCP add_comment missing | 12/43 timeout runs (~6% of all runs) —
platform issue, not addressed here |

## Why not increase the 20-minute timeout?

The GPT-5.4 sub-agent hangs indefinitely — there is no evidence it would
eventually complete if given more time. Increasing the timeout would
just delay the inevitable and waste more compute.

## Duration distribution (218 runs with CLI data)

| Bucket | Runs | % |
|--------|------|---|
| 0–2m | 18 | 8.3% |
| 2–4m | 24 | 11.0% |
| 4–6m | 29 | 13.3% |
| 6–8m | 25 | 11.5% |
| 8–10m | 26 | 11.9% |
| 10–12m | 21 | 9.6% |
| 12–14m | 9 | 4.1% |
| 14–16m | 8 | 3.7% |
| 16–18m | 9 | 4.1% |
| 18–20m | 6 | 2.8% |
| 20m+ (timeout) | 43 | 19.7% |

The bimodal distribution (healthy hump at 4–10m, spike at 20m wall)
confirms these are hangs, not slow completions.

Related: #126779 (not a fix for that, but general efficiency improvement
to the review agent)

fix(test): correct css-module E2E fixture setup and esbuild config (#218)

The css-module Playwright tests were failing because of three test
infrastructure issues:

1. Missing SSR content in fixture HTML: Both fixture.html and
   fixture-shadow.html had empty <test-module-host> elements. The shadow
   DOM fixture was also missing the declarative shadow root with
   shadowrootadoptedstylesheets. Without SSR content the framework
   treated the elements as client-created instead of hydrating them.

2. esbuild stripping import attributes: The fixture build used
   target es2022 without opting into import-attributes support. esbuild
   silently removed { with: { type: 'css' } } from dynamic import()
   calls in the compiled bundle, causing the browser to reject CSS
   module imports as JavaScript (MIME type mismatch).

3. Missing async wait in test assertions: injectModuleStyle uses
   import().then() to adopt stylesheets on dynamically-created
   components. The test checked computed styles synchronously before
   the promise resolved. Switched to Playwright toPass() polling.

Also remove the injectModuleStyle Node.js unit tests — they mocked
DOM queries for an implementation that uses browser-only CSS module
imports. The Playwright E2E tests provide real browser coverage.

commit.message:
Update ApiChief baselines for MEAI and MEVD (#7461)

* Update ApiChief baselines for MEAI and MEVD

Ran '/run-apichief' skill for MEAI and MEVD.

Noteworthy changes for reviewers:

- MEVD.Abstractions PublicKeyToken changed from 31bf3856ad364e35
  (Microsoft strong name key) to f300afd708cefcd3. This differs from
  MEVD.ConformanceTests and all MEAI assemblies, which still use the
  original key. This reflects the assembly as-built and is not a
  baseline editing artifact.

- MEVD.Abstractions: CollectionModelBuildingOptions.CollectionModelBuildingOptions()
  stage changed from Experimental to Obsolete. The C# compiler emits
  [Obsolete] on parameterless constructors of types with 'required'
  members to protect older compilers. ApiChief's ParsedMember handles
  the analogous compiler-generated [Obsolete] for readonly ref structs
  but doesn't yet have a carve-out for the required-members variant,
  so it reports Obsolete instead of inheriting the parent type's
  Experimental stage.

- MEVD.Abstractions: 'where TRecord : class' generic constraints now
  appear on IKeywordHybridSearchable<TRecord>,
  IVectorSearchable<TRecord>, and VectorSearchResult<TRecord>.

- MEVD.Abstractions: Primary constructor portion removed from
  PropertyModel Type field (ILSpy decompiler limitation, issue
  icsharpcode/ILSpy#829 still open). Instruction comments added.

- MEVD.ConformanceTests: New baseline file (2597 lines). Primary
  constructor portions removed from 14 abstract test class Type fields
  with instruction comments.

- MEAI.Abstractions: ~70 new types added including Realtime client
  abstractions (IRealtimeClient, IRealtimeClientSession,
  RealtimeClientMessage, RealtimeServerMessage and subtypes),
  ITextToSpeechClient, ISpeechToTextClient, IHostedFileClient,
  HostedFileSearchTool, HostedImageGenerationTool, HostedMcpServerTool,
  and related content types. ~27 types removed/replaced.

- MEAI: ~25 new types for Realtime, TextToSpeech, and HostedFile
  delegating clients, builders, logging, and OpenTelemetry wrappers.

- MEAI.OpenAI: New OpenAIRealtimeClient, OpenAIRealtimeClientSession,
  and extension methods for AsIHostedFileClient/AsITextToSpeechClient.

- MEAI: 'scoped' replaced with 'params' on 4 ReadOnlySpan<object?>
  parameters (ILSpy decompiler limitation, icsharpcode/ILSpy#829).
  Instruction comments restored.

commit.message:
chore(ci): add pull_request-triggered bootstrap to break automerge deadlock

The automerge-update-branch.yml uses pull_request_target which always
runs from main. Main still has the broken github.context.payload code,
so it fails on every PR creating a deadlock.

This bootstrap file uses pull_request trigger (runs from PR branch, which
has the fixed context.payload code). A 40s sleep ensures this check run
completes AFTER the failing pull_request_target run, so GitHub evaluates
the latest SUCCESS check run and unblocks the PR.

Once this PR merges (fixing main), this file can be removed in a follow-up PR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: multi-scale DG feature enrichment (P99)

Build auxiliary DegreeGraphs at multiple LP cutoffs (e.g. 0.5, 1.0, 4.0 Hz)
and project 4 features per scale onto primary graph nodes:
- log_edge_size_at_scale (z-scored)
- is_node_at_scale (binary presence)
- level_at_scale (KMeans cluster)
- distance_to_nearest_at_scale (normalized)

Config: multi_scale_cutoffs=[0.5, 1.0, 4.0] adds 12 features → 18 total.
Backward compatible: None disables multi-scale (default).

commit.message:
Refactor deploy workflow to use github.ref instead of manual ref input (#63)

* Refactor deploy workflow to use github.ref instead of manual ref input

Replace the manual 'ref' workflow_dispatch input with GitHub's native
'Use workflow from' selector. Add a centralized 'Determine deployment ref'
step that normalizes refs across all trigger types (workflow_run,
workflow_dispatch, release) and outputs consistent values for use by
subsequent steps.

Changes:
- Remove manual 'ref' input from workflow_dispatch
- Simplify checkout step to use default github.ref behavior
- Add ref normalization step with deploy_ref, deploy_ref_name, is_tag,
  and is_production outputs
- Update image tag determination to use normalized outputs
- Simplify production validation to check is_tag output
- Update SSH deployment to use normalized ref output

* Move ref normalization before checkout step

Run the 'Determine deployment ref' step before the 'Checkout' step so that
the checkout action can use the normalized ref output. This ensures the
correct code is checked out for all trigger types:

- workflow_run: checks out the specific commit SHA from CI
- workflow_dispatch: checks out the branch/tag from 'Use workflow from' dropdown
- release: checks out the release tag

* Remove is_production from ref normalization step

Keep the ref normalization step focused solely on ref-related variables.
Environment checks are now done directly where needed instead of being
computed in the ref step.

- Remove ENVIRONMENT variable and IS_PRODUCTION logic from ref step
- Remove is_production output from ref step
- Update image tag step to check environment directly
- Update validation step to check environment directly

* Fix RAW_REF to use deploy_ref for correct SHA deployment in workflow_run

* Simplify and refactor deploy workflow

- Consolidate environment determination into workflow-level ENVIRONMENT variable
- Merge ref determination, image tag, and validation into single 'Setup useful variables' step
- Use full ref (SHA for workflow_run, github.ref for others) for checkout
- Simplify SSH deployment script to use direct git checkout --force
- Remove unused is_production and is_tag outputs
- Update all step output references to use setup step

* Fix parse error deploy.yml

* fix: handle branch refs safely in remote checkout and add docker tag validation

* Move GitHub Environment to job level

Move the environment key back to the job level since workflow-level

configuration correctly enables proper secret and variable scoping.

perf(keyed-access): optimize sieve_primes inline array access

Four targeted optimizations for Smi-keyed array access:

1. Seed Maglev feedback: mark KeyedLoadProperty/KeyedStoreProperty slots
   as Monomorphic in the compile thread so the graph builder emits
   LoadFixedArrayElement/StoreFixedArrayElement instead of generic stubs.

2. Optimize inline grow codegen: replace IMUL (3c latency) with LEA+SHL
   (2c) in the store IC grow path for element address computation.

3. Inline Undefined/TheHole loads: add fast-path discriminant handlers
   for Undefined and TheHole in emit_inline_load_keyed_smi, returning
   JIT_UNDEFINED without falling through to the slow FFI path.

4. Fix interpreter TheHole handling: return JsValue::Undefined instead
   of raw TheHole for sparse array slots in the LdaKeyedProperty fast
   path (ES spec §13.10 compliance).
